environment.libradtranSource: conda-forge rubin-libradtran (real build of the upstream libRadtran 2.0.6 tarball)

checks.detail: sim 10^2.12 cd/m2 vs anchor 10^2.30 ± 0.45 dex (Koomen 1952 / Rozenberg 1966 zenith luminance at sunset ~1e2..4e2 cd/m2)
sim 10^0.70 cd/m2 vs anchor 10^0.78 ± 0.55 dex (interpolated Koomen/Rozenberg zenith decay (~1 dex per ~2 deg))
sim 10^-1.25 cd/m2 vs anchor 10^-1.22 ± 0.65 dex (interpolated Koomen/Rozenberg zenith decay toward ~1e-3 cd/m2 at ~11 deg)

checks.check: zenith decay slope 4-8 deg vs Patat 2006 (~1 mag/deg, 6-12 deg)
checks.detail: sim 1.22 mag/arcsec2/deg vs literature 0.8-1.2 (ranges differ: 4-8 vs 6-12 deg — tolerance widened ±0.25)

checks.check: all resolved radiances finite and positive
brightness declines monotonically with depression (12 geometries)
sunward brighter than antisolar at alt 10

checks.detail: monotone growth expected as shadow rises antisolar-first

checks.check: AOD response finite and documented (dep 4, alt 10, sunward)

diagnostics.detail: sim 919 lx (10^2.96) vs anchor 10^2.74 ± 0.3 (sunset horizontal illuminance ~400-700 lx)
sim 37.8 lx (10^1.58) vs anchor 10^1.08 ± 0.45 (interpolated twilight illuminance curve)
sim 0.618 lx (10^-0.21) vs anchor 10^-0.46 ± 0.55 (interpolated twilight illuminance curve)

gates.evidence: MYSTIC 1D-spherical backward produces finite positive radiance for depressions 0-8 deg; DISORT/pseudospherical demonstrated invalid (negative radiances) beyond SZA 90 and is not used

gates.gate: numerical stability adequate for a LUT
runtime practical for the expanded grid
output convertible to the calculator's quantities
gates.evidence: spectral radiance -> photopic luminance -> nL -> KS-convention mag/arcsec2; fine-vs-node grid consistency median 0.32%
9/9 anchor+sanity checks passed (offline literature anchors; see OBSERVATIONAL_VALIDATION.md)

supportedDomain.sunDepressionNote: 0-8 deg resolves to <=6% rel. error at the boosted photon budgets; 9-10 deg resolves to ~9-13% at zenith with 4e7 photons (domain probes); 12 deg is MARGINAL (~37%). Worst geometry (low altitude, near-sun) is noisier than zenith at equal depth. Practical LUT limit ~10 deg; 10-12 deg needs >1e8 photons.
supportedDomain.unsupported: depression > ~12 deg (MC cost impractical), altitude < 10 deg (untested), any cloud, refraction not modelled, single aerosol family / atmosphere only
